latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
Today 10:20
RNS Number : 2234C
Videndum PLC
28 April 2026
28 April 2026
VIDENDUM PLC
Notification of Transactions of Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibility (PDMRs)
Restricted Share Plan ("RSP") - 2026 Awards
Videndum plc ("the Company") announces that on 24 April 2026 the following awards of ordinary shares of one pence each were made under the Company's RSP to Executive Directors and PDMRs.
Name
Position
Award of 2026 RSP
Stephen Harris
Chairman
125,786
Brian Morgan
Chief Financial Officer
201,256
The award to Stephen Harris represents 100% of salary. Brian Morgan's award represents 200 per cent of salary, comprising a 100 per cent of salary normal annual award (as per that granted to Stephen Harris) and 100 per cent of salary recruitment award. The recruitment award was agreed to secure Brian Morgan's recruitment at his appointment in October 2025 albeit the grant was: (i) delayed given the need to focus on the refinancing of the Company; and (ii) contingent on investing £50,000 of own funds into the equity raise and retaining the resulting shares during the vesting period.
The RSP awards will vest after three years from grant subject to the Director remaining with Videndum until the vesting date and the Remuneration Committee being satisfied that the vesting is fair and reasonable against the Company's underlying financial performance.
There are no other performance conditions tied to the 2026 RSP award, and the award is consistent with the Company's Remuneration Policy.
Enquiries:
Jon Bolton
Group Company Secretary
020 8332 4602
A snapshot of Videndum plc
Videndum is a leading global provider of premium branded hardware products and software solutions to the content creation market.
Our product portfolio includes camera supports, video transmission systems and monitors, live streaming solutions, robotic camera systems, prompters, LED lighting, mobile power, bags, backgrounds, audio capture, and noise reduction equipment.
We employ around 1,200 people across the world in 9 different countries. Videndum plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange, ticker: VID.
More information can be found at:
https://videndum.com/
LEI number: 2138007H5DQ4X8YOCF14

latest.content:
Today 13:06
RNS Number : 8654Z
Videndum PLC
09 April 2026
9 April 2026
Videndum plc
Admission to Trading & TVR
Further to the announcement by Videndum plc (the "Company") on 8 April 2026 regarding Transactions by Directors and Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities (PDMRs), 171,116 New Ordinary Shares have been issued and allotted to PDMRs and Senior Managers, and are expected to be admitted to trading on 10 April 2026
.
The total issued ordinary share capital of the Company following admission will be 40,294,123 Ordinary Shares and the total number of voting rights of the Company will be 40,294,123. This figure may be used by Shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change in their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: VID.L is a small‑cap technology outfit that builds and sells video‑streaming infrastructure for businesses and broadcasters. Its core offering is a cloud‑based platform that lets customers embed live and on‑demand video into their own websites and apps, along with analytics and monetisation tools.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Video traffic is exploding worldwide, and enterprises are looking for flexible, scalable ways to host and deliver content without building their own servers. VID.L’s platform sits at the intersection of cloud services and media delivery, giving it a foothold in a market that is growing at double‑digit rates and where switching costs are still low.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a very early revenue phase and has recently completed a capital‑raising round that diluted existing shareholders. It is still working to move from prototype to a product that can generate cash flow. The share price is trading near the bottom of its recent range, reflecting the market’s lack of confidence in its near‑term viability.

rns_narrative: VID.L has been busy restructuring its board and raising fresh capital to shore up cash. The latest filings show a director change, a correction to shareholding figures, and a formal notice of an AGM that will discuss the new capital structure.

The company released its 2025 full‑year results and a comprehensive refinancing plan aimed at extending the runway. It also announced the launch of an open offer and a prospectus for a firm placing, signalling an intention to bring in institutional investors.

Overall, the narrative is one of survival and preparation for a potential turnaround: the company is tightening its balance sheet and positioning itself to attract new funding or a strategic partnership.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence with a few angry voices – most traders are ignoring the ticker, while a handful of short‑sellers are warning about dilution and weak fundamentals.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are no clear bullish voices yet. The only bullish chatter comes from a single trader who believes that a successful funding round could lift the stock to new highs.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears focus on the dilution hit, the lack of revenue, and the fact that the company is still in a pre‑profit stage. They argue that the price has already punished the market and that the company might not recover.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has stayed flat over the last month – no new positive buzz, and the few negative posts remain unchanged. The community is essentially on standby, waiting for a catalyst that might break the quiet.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for VID.L is dead. No search interest has been recorded, which is a classic signal that the market is not yet aware of any upcoming surprise.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming AGM could approve a new capital‑raising plan that would bring in institutional capital and improve liquidity.
A potential partnership announcement with a larger media or cloud player would validate the product’s market fit and could trigger a price rally.
If the company delivers a milestone in its product roadmap – for example, a beta launch to a key customer – that could spark buying interest from early adopters.
Regulatory approval for a new data‑privacy feature could open up a new customer base and drive demand.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in daily trading volume (above 10% of average volume) coupled with a break of the 20‑day moving average. A clear support level around the mid‑point of the 52‑week range that holds for two consecutive days would also be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a post‑crash washout – the price has fallen sharply and the market is still digesting the dilution event. A strong catalyst is needed to kick off mean reversion; until then, the risk of a further slide remains.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share has fallen from its peak by roughly 80‑90%, putting it in the deep‑crash zone where historical data shows a high probability of a bounce. If the stock recovers to the mid‑point of its 52‑week range, the upside is about 170 % from the current level. A full rebound to the previous peak would deliver more than 400 % gains. The asymmetry is strong: the upside far outweighs the downside.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure fresh capital or a strategic partnership, it could stay stuck at the bottom of its range or even slide to zero. The worst‑case scenario is a further dilution of the already thin equity base, making it difficult for the company to survive without external funding.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Assuming a stop at a level that caps losses at roughly 40 % of the entry price, the potential upside of 170‑400 % gives a risk‑reward ratio of at least 4:1. Even with a modest 100‑day stop, the asymmetry remains attractive.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter near the current support level, sizing the position so that a 40 % loss would be the maximum you’re willing to accept. Place a stop just below the 20‑day moving average to give the trade room to breathe. Target the mid‑point of the 52‑week range first, then trail the stop as the price climbs toward the former peak.
playbook.if_watching: Hold off until you see a volume surge of at least 10 % over the 20‑day average and a clean break above the 20‑day moving average. Also monitor the AGM minutes for any new funding announcement; a positive outcome would justify a quick entry.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted from the LSE, files for bankruptcy, or the AGM approves a liquidation plan, exit immediately. These terminal risks would wipe out any upside potential.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep‑crash setup with dead market chatter suggests a high‑reward, low‑downside play if a funding or partnership catalyst materialises within the next quarter.
